title: Make Small Talk
summary: Start and maintain a light, friendly conversation with someone you do not know well.
learnerPromise: You will be able to start a friendly conversation with strangers or acquaintances and keep it going comfortably for a few minutes.
whyItMatters: Small talk opens doors. It helps you meet new people, feel more comfortable in social settings, and build connections that can grow into friendships or opportunities.
realLifeUses: Chatting with a classmate while waiting for a lecture to start
Talking to a cashier or barista while checking out
Starting a conversation at a social event or party
Breaking the ice with a new coworker in the break room
result: A short, friendly conversation started and maintained with someone new
youWillLearn: Start a conversation with a simple observation or question
Use open-ended questions to keep the other person talking
Find common ground through shared topics like weather, classes, or events
Keep the conversation light and know when to wrap it up
miniChallenge: Start a short conversation with someone you do not know well. Use an observation or open question, ask one follow-up, and find one thing you have in common.
steps: Choose a safe, shared topic like the setting, weather, or something you both see
Start with a simple question or comment to open the conversation
Ask an open-ended follow-up question that cannot be answered with just yes or no
Listen and find one point of connection to comment on
Keep it brief and positive, then let the conversation end naturally
completionCriteria: You initiated a conversation with someone you do not know well
The exchange lasted at least two to three minutes
You asked at least one open-ended question and found a shared topic
commonProblems: Asking questions that can be answered with just yes or no, which kills the flow
Talking only about yourself without asking the other person anything
Feeling pressure to be interesting instead of being interested
tips: People love talking about things they care about; your job is to be curious
Comments about the shared environment are the easiest conversation starters
It is okay if the conversation is short; even two minutes of friendly talk counts
